audience: Belgian self-employed professionals and SMEs that want a faster, more usable website and a sound understanding of its SEO impact
primaryDutchSearchIntent: Uitleggen hoe websitesnelheid, Core Web Vitals, technische kwaliteit, gebruikerservaring en SEO samenhangen zonder rankinggaranties

searchIntent.question: Can a faster website rank better, and which performance improvements should an SME prioritise?

longTailKeywords: why a fast website can rank better
directAnswer: A fast website can support better search visibility because Core Web Vitals contribute to Google's broader page experience assessment. Speed alone never guarantees rankings, so SMEs should improve real user experience alongside relevant content, accessibility and technical quality.
title: Why a fast website can rank better
metaDescription: Does website speed help SEO? Learn what Core Web Vitals measure, how Google assesses speed and which improvements Belgian SMEs should prioritise.
h1: Why a fast website can rank better
factsToPreserve: Google states that good Core Web Vitals do not guarantee top rankings
The stable Core Web Vitals are LCP, INP and CLS
Good thresholds are LCP at most 2.5 seconds, INP at most 200 milliseconds and CLS at most 0.1 at the 75th percentile
CrUX field data uses a rolling 28-day period
Mobile and desktop are assessed separately
The article distinguishes field data, lab data and first-party real-user monitoring
No fixed conversion or ranking improvement is promised
